adhkar.arabicText: اللَّهُمَّ لاَ طَيْرَ إِلاَّ طَيْرُكَ، وَلاَ خَيْرَ إِلاَّ خَيْرُكَ، وَلاَ إِلَهَ غَيْرُكَ.
adhkar.transliteration: This supplication is used whenever one initially thinks a casual event or occurrence to foretell good or evil, using it as a basis to determine which action he should undertake, but he then denounces such a link, relies on Allah and then says this supplication as an expiation for this act, since it falls under the category of shirk.)(Allahumma la tayra illa tayruk, wala khayra illa khayruk, wala ilaha ghayruk
adhkar.translation: This supplication is used whenever one initially thinks a casual event or occurrence to foretell good or evil, using it as a basis to determine which action he should undertake, but he then denounces such a link, relies on Allah and then says this supplication as an expiation for this act, since it falls under the category of shirk. (O Allah, there is no omen but there is reliance on You, there is no good except Your good and none has the right to be worshipped except You.)
adhkar.sourceReference: [TO DO]

adhkar.title: In the name of Allah and all praise...
adhkar.arabicText: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ، وَالْحَمْدُ للَّهِ ﴿سُبْحَانَ الَّذِي سَخَّرَ لَنَا هَذَا وَمَا كُنَّا لَهُ مُقْرِنِينَ، وَإِنَّا إِلَى رَبِّنَا لَمُنقَلِبُونَ﴾، الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ، الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ، الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، سُبْحَانَكَ اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي ظَلَمْتُ نَفْسِي فَاغْفِرْ لِي؛ فَإِنَّهُ لاَ يَغْفِرُ الذُّنُوبَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ.
adhkar.transliteration: Bismil-lah, walhamdu lillah, subhanal-lathee sakhkhara lana hatha wama kunna lahu muqrineen, wainna ila rabbina lamunqaliboon, alhamdu lillah, alhamdu lillah, alhamdu lillah, Allahu akbar, Allahu akbar, Allahu akbar, subhanakal-lahumma innee thalamtu nafsee faghfir lee fainnahu la yaghfiruth-thunooba illa ant
adhkar.translation: In the name of Allah and all praise is for Allah. How perfect He is, the One Who has placed this (transport. at our service and we ourselves would not have been capable of that, and to our Lord is our final destiny. All praise is for Allah, All praise is for Allah, All praise is for Allah, Allah is the greatest, Allah is the greatest, Allah is the greatest. How perfect You are, O Allah, verily I have wronged my soul, so forgive me, for surely none can forgive sins except You.

adhkar.title: Allah is the greatest, Allah is the greatest...
adhkar.arabicText: اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، ﴿سُبْحَانَ الَّذِي سَخَّرَ لَنَا هَذَا وَمَا كُنَّا لَهُ مُقْرِنِينَ وَإِنَّا إِلَى رَبِّنَا لَمُنقَلِبُونَ﴾ ((اللَّهُمَّ إِنّا نَسْأَلُكَ فِي سَفَرِنَا هَذَا البِرَّ وَالتَّقْوَى، وَمِنَ الْعَمَلِ مَا تَرْضَى، اللَّهُمَّ هَوِّنْ عَلَيْنَا سَفَرَنَا هَذَا وَاطْوِ عَنَّا بُعْدَهُ، اللَّهُمَّ أَنْتَ الصَّاحِبُ فِي السَّفَرِ، وَالْخَليفَةُ فِي الْأَهْلِ، اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ وَعْثَاءِ السَّفَرِ، وَكَآبَةِ الْمَنْظَرِ، وَسُوءِ الْمُنْقَلَبِ فِي الْمَالِ وَالْأَهْلِ). ، وإذا رَجَعَ قَالَهُنَّ وَزَادَ فِيهِنَّ: (آيِبُونَ، تائِبُونَ، عَابِدُونَ، لِرَبِّنَا حَامِدُونَ)).
adhkar.transliteration: Allahu akbar, Allahu akbar, Allahu akbar, subhanal-lathee sakhkhara lana hatha wama kunna lahu muqrineen, wa-inna ila rabbina lamunqaliboon, allahumma inna nas-aluka fee safarina hatha albirra wattaqwa, waminal-AAamali ma tarda, allahumma hawwin AAalayna safarana hatha, watwi AAanna buAAdah, allahumma antas-sahibu fis-safar, walkhaleefatu fil-ahl, allahumma innee aAAoothu bika min waAAtha-is-safar, waka-abatil-manthar, wasoo-il-munqalabi fil-mali wal-ahl.)(upon returning the same supplication is recited with the following addition: (Ayiboona, ta-iboona, AAabidoona, lirabbina hamidoon
adhkar.translation: Allah is the greatest, Allah is the greatest, Allah is the greatest, How perfect He is, The One Who has placed this (transport. at our service, and we ourselves would not have been capable of that, and to our Lord is our final destiny. O Allah, we ask You for birr and taqwa in this journey of ours, and we ask You for deeds which please You. O Allah, facilitate our journey and let us cover it’s distance quickly. O Allah, You are The Companion on the journey and The Successor over the family, O Allah, I take refuge with You from the difficulties of travel, from having a change of hearts and being in a bad predicament, and I take refuge in You from an ill fated outcome with wealth and family. (birr and taqwa: two comprehensive terms which individually, refer to all good actions and obedience i.e. performing the commanded actions and avoiding the prohibited actions. When combined together, birr refers to doing those actions which have been commanded and taqwa refers to avoiding those actions which have been prohibited.)(A successor: one who succeeds another due to the latter’s absence or death. This is the correct meaning of the word khaleefah; thus, it is incorrect to believe that Adam is the khaleefah (vicegerent, as is commonly translated. of Allah on earth because Allah is never absent, and will never die. This supplication proves the correct understanding of this term and shows that Allah succeeds us and guards whom we leave behind when we die or are absent.)(upon returning the same supplication is recited with the following addition: We return, repent, worship and praise our Lord.))

adhkar.title: O Allah, Lord of the seven heavens and...
adhkar.arabicText: اللَّهُمَّ رَبَّ السَّمَوَاتِ السَّبْعِ وَمَا أَظْلَلْنَ، وَرَبَّ الأَرَضِينَ السَّبْعِ وَمَا أَقْلَلْنَ، وَرَبَّ الشَّياطِينِ وَمَا أَضْلَلْنَ، وَرَبَّ الرِّيَاحِ وَمَا ذَرَيْنَ، أَسْأَلُكَ خَيْرَ هَذِهِ الْقَرْيَةِ، وَخَيْرَ أَهْلِهَا، وَخَيْرَ مَا فِيهَا، وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّهَا، وَشَرِّ أَهْلِهَا، وَشَرِّ مَا فِيهَا.
adhkar.transliteration: Allahumma rabbas-samawatis-sabAAi wama athlaln, warabbal-aradeenas-sabAAi wama aqlaln, warabbash-shayateeni wama adlaln, warabbar-riyahi wama tharayn, as-aluka khayra hathihil-qaryah, wakhayra ahlilha wakhayra ma feeha, wa-aAAoothu bika min sharriha washarri ahliha, washarri ma feeha
adhkar.translation: O Allah, Lord of the seven heavens and all that they envelop, Lord of the seven earths and all that they carry, Lord of the devils and all whom they misguide, Lord of the winds and all whom they whisk away. I ask You for the goodness of this village, the goodness of its inhabitants and for all the goodness found within it and I take refuge with You from the evil of this village, the evil of its inhabitants and from all the evil found within it.

adhkar.title: None has the right to be worshipped except...
adhkar.arabicText: لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ، وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ، يُحْيِي وَيُمِيتُ، وَهُوَ حَيٌّ لاِ يَمُوتُ، بِيَدِهِ الْخَيْرُ، وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ.
adhkar.transliteration: La ilaha illal-lah, wahdahu la shareeka lah, lahul-mulku walahul-hamd, yuhyee wayumeetu wahuwa hayyun la yamoot, biyadihil-khayru wahuwa AAala kulli shayin qadeer
adhkar.translation: None has the right to be worshipped except Allah, alone, without partner, to Him belongs all sovereignty and praise. He gives life and causes death, and He is living and does not die. In His hand is all good and He is over all things, omnipotent.

adhkar.title: Bismil-lah.
adhkar.arabicText: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ.
adhkar.transliteration: Bismi allhi
adhkar.translation: In the name of Allah.
adhkar.sourceReference: Al-Tirmidhī (#606, 2/505) and Sahīh al-Jāmiʿ (#3210, 3/203). Also see Irwāʾ al-Ghalīl (#50)

adhkar.title: I place you in the trust of Allah...
adhkar.arabicText: أَسْتَوْدِعُكُمُ اللَّهَ الَّذِي لاَ تَضِيعُ وَدَائِعُهُ.
adhkar.transliteration: AstawdiAAukumul-lah, allathee la tadeeAAu wada-iAAuh
adhkar.translation: I place you in the trust of Allah, whose trust is never misplaced.

adhkar.title: I place your religion, your faithfulness and the...
adhkar.arabicText: أَسْتَوْدِعُ اللَّهَ دِينَكَ، وَأَمَانَتَكَ، وَخَوَاتِيمَ عَمَلِكَ.
adhkar.transliteration: AstawdiAAul-laha deenak, wa-amanatak, wakhawateema AAamalik
adhkar.translation: I place your religion, your faithfulness and the ends of your deeds in the trust of Allah.
adhkar.sourceReference: Aḥmad (2/7) and al-Tirmidhī (#3443, 5/499). Also see Ṣaḥīḥ al-Tirmidhī (2/155)

adhkar.title: Zawwadakal-lahut-taqwa, waghafara thanbak, wayassara lakal-khayra haythuma kunt.
adhkar.arabicText: زَوَّدَكَ اللَّهُ التَّقْوَى، وَغَفَرَ ذَنْبَكَ، وَيَسَّرَ لَكَ الخَيْرَ حَيْثُ ما كُنْتَ.
adhkar.transliteration: Zawwadaka alllahu alttaqwaa, waghafaradhanbaka, wayassara laka alkhayra haythumaa kunta
adhkar.translation: May Allah endow you with taqwa, forgive your sins and facilitate all good for you, wherever you be. (taqwa: a comprehensive term which refers to all good actions and obedience i.e. performing the commanded actions and avoiding the prohibited actions.)
adhkar.sourceReference: Al-Tirmidhī (#3444). Also see Ṣaḥīḥ al-Tirmidhī (3/155)

adhkar.title: Glorifying and magnifying Allah on the journey
adhkar.arabicText: قَالَ جَابِرٌ رضي الله عنه: (كُنَّا إِذَا صَعَدْنَا كَبَّرْنَا، وَإِذَا نَزَلْنَا سَبَّحْنَا)).
adhkar.transliteration: Qaala jaabirun rdy Allah 'nh: ((kunnaa iidhaa sa'adnaa kabbarnaa, waiidhaa nazalnaa sabbahnaa
adhkar.translation: Jabir said: Whenever we went up a hill we would say Allaahu 'Akber (Allah is the Most Great), and when we descended we would say Subhaanallaah (Glory is to Allah).

adhkar.title: May a witness, be witness to our praise...
adhkar.arabicText: سَمَّعَ سَامِعٌ بِحَمْدِ اللَّهِ، وَحُسْنِ بَلاَئِهِ عَلَيْنَا، رَبَّنَا صاحِبْنَا، وَأَفْضِلْ عَلَيْنَا، عَائِذاً بِاللَّهِ مِنَ النَّارِ.
adhkar.transliteration: SamiAAa samiAAun bihamdil-lahi wahusni bala-ihi AAalayna. Rabbana sahibna wa-afdil AAalayna AAa-ithan billahi minan-nar
adhkar.translation: May a witness, be witness to our praise of Allah for His favours and bounties upon us. Our Lord, protect us, show favour on us and deliver us from every evil. I take refuge in Allah from the fire.

adhkar.title: I take refuge in Allah’s perfect words from...
adhkar.arabicText: أَعُوذُ بِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ التَّامَّاتِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ.
adhkar.transliteration: aAAoothu bikalimatil-lahit-tammati min sharri ma khalaq
adhkar.translation: I take refuge in Allah’s perfect words from the evil that He has created.

adhkar.title: Ibn AAumar reported that the Messenger of Allah...
adhkar.arabicText: يُكَبِّرُ عَلَى كُلِّ شَرَفٍ ثَلاَثَ تَكْبِيرَاتٍ ثُمَّ يَقُولُ: لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ، وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ، وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ، آيِبُونَ، تَائِبُونَ، عَابِدُونَ، لِرَبِّنا حَامِدُونَ، صَدَقَ اللَّهُ وَعْدَهُ، وَنَصَرَ عَبْدَهُ، وَهَزَمَ الْأَحْزابَ وَحْدَهُ.
adhkar.transliteration: Ibn AAumar reported that the Messenger of Allah on return from a battle or from performing the pilgrimage would say at every high point: (Allahu akbar, Allahu akbar, Allahu akbar.) and then he would say: (La ilaha illal-lahu wahdahu la shareeka lah, lahul-mulku walahul-hamd, wahuwa AAala kulli shay-in qadeer, ayiboona ta-iboon, AAabidoon, lirabbina hamidoon, sadaqal-lahu waAAdah, wanasara AAabdah, wahazamal-ahzaba wahdah
adhkar.translation: Ibn AAumar reported that the Messenger of Allah on return from a battle or from performing the pilgrimage would say at every high point: Allah is the greatest, Allah is the greatest, Allah is the greatest, and then he would say: None has the right to be worshipped except Allah, alone, without partner. To Him belongs all sovereignty and praise, and He is over all things omnipotent. We return, repent, worship and praise our Lord. Allah fulfilled His promise, aided His Servant, and single-handedly defeated the allies.

adhkar.title: When something happened that pleased him, the Prophet...
adhkar.arabicText: كَانَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم إِذَا أَتَاهُ الْأَمْرُ يَسُرُّهُ قَالَ: (الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي بِنِعْمَتِهِ تَتِمُّ الصَّالِحَاتُ). وَإِذَا أَتَاهُ الْأَمْرُ يَكْرَهُهُ قَالَ: (الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ عَلَى كُلِّ حَالٍ)).
adhkar.transliteration: When something happened that pleased him, the Prophet used to say:(Alhamdu lillaahil-lathee bini'matihi tatimmuus-saalihaat).And if something happened that displeased him, he used to say:(Alhamdulillaahi 'alaa kulli haal
adhkar.translation: When something happened that pleased him, the Prophet used to say: (Alhamdu lillaahil-lathee bini'matihi tatimmuus-saalihaat)). Praise is to Allah Who by His blessings all good things are perfected. And if something happened that displeased him, he used to say: (Alhamdulillaahi 'alaa kulli haal)).Praise is to Allah in all circumstances.

adhkar.title: The Prophet (PBUH) said: ‘Whoever sends a prayer...
adhkar.arabicText: قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم: (مَنْ صَلَّى عَلَيَّ صَلاَةً صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ بِهَا عَشْراً)).
adhkar.transliteration: Qaala alnnabiyyu sla Allah 'lyh wslm: ((man sallaa 'alayya salahan sallaa alllahu 'alayhi bihaa 'ashraan
adhkar.translation: The Prophet (PBUH. said: ‘Whoever sends a prayer upon me, Allah sends ten upon him.

adhkar.title: He (PBUH) also said: ‘Do not take my...
adhkar.arabicText: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: (لاَ تَجْعَلُوا قَبْرِي عِيداً وَصَلُّوا عَلَيَّ؛ فَإِنَّ صَلاَتَكُم تَبْلُغُنِي حَيْثُ كُنْتُمْ)).
adhkar.transliteration: Waqaala sla Allah 'lyh wslm: ((la taj'aluwa qabriy 'iydaan wasalluwa 'alayya; faiinna salatakum tablughuniy haythu kuntum
adhkar.translation: He (PBUH. also said: ‘Do not take my grave as a place of habitual ceremony. Send prayers upon me, for verily your prayers reach me wherever you are.

adhkar.title: He (PBUH) also said: ‘A miser is one...
adhkar.arabicText: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: (الْبَخِيلُ مَنْ ذُكِرْتُ عِنْدَهُ فَلَمْ يُصَلِّ عَلَيَّ)).
adhkar.transliteration: Waqaala sla Allah 'lyh wslm: ((albakhiylu man dhukirtu 'indahu falam yusalli 'alayya
adhkar.translation: He (PBUH. also said: ‘A miser is one whom when I am mentioned to him, fails to send prayers upon me.

adhkar.title: The Prophet PBUH said:'Indeed Allah has angels who...
adhkar.arabicText: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: (إِنَّ لِلَّهِ مَلاَئِكَةً سَيَّاحِينَ فِي الْأَرْضِ يُبَلِّغُونِي مِنْ أُمَّتِي السَّلاَمَ)).
adhkar.transliteration: Waqaala sla Allah 'lyh wslm:((iinna lillahi malaiikahan sayyaahiyna fi alardi yuballighuwniy min aummatiy alssalama
adhkar.translation: The Prophet PBUH said:'Indeed Allah has angels who roam the earth and they convey to me the greetings (or prayers of peace. of my 'Ummah (nation).

adhkar.title: The Prophet PBUH said: 'No one sends greetings...
adhkar.arabicText: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: (مَا مِنْ أَحَدٍ يُسَلِّمُ عَلَيَّ إِلاَّ رَدَّ اللَّهُ عَلَيَّ رُوحِيَ حَتَّى أَرُدَّ عَلَيْهِ السَّلاَمَ)).
adhkar.transliteration: Waqaala sla Allah 'lyh wslm: ((maa min ahadin yusallimu 'alayya iila radda alllahu 'alayya ruwhiya hattaa arudda 'alayhi alssalama
adhkar.translation: The Prophet PBUH said: 'No one sends greetings (or prayers of peace. upon me but Allah returns my soul to me so that I may return his greetings

adhkar.title: The Messenger of Allah said: ‘You shall not...
adhkar.arabicText: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم: (لاَ تَدْخُلُوا الْجَنَّةَ حَتَّى تُؤْمِنُوا، وَلاَ تُؤْمِنُوا حَتَّى تَحَابُّوا، أَوَلاَ أَدُلُّكُم عَلَى شَيْءٍ إِذَا فَعَلْتُمُوهُ تَحَابَبْتُم، أَفْشُوا السَّلاَمَ بَيْنَكُمْ)).
adhkar.transliteration: Qaala rasuwlu alllahi sla Allah 'lyh wslm: ((la tadkhuluwa aljannaha hattaa tuuminuwa, wala tuuminuwa hattaa tahaabbuwa, awala adullukum 'alaa shay'in iidhaa fa'altumuwhu tahaababtum, afshuwa alssalama baynakum
adhkar.translation: The Messenger of Allah said: ‘You shall not enter paradise until you believe, and you shall not believe until you love one another. Shall I not inform you of something, if you were to act upon it, you will indeed achieve mutual love for one another? Spread the greeting amongst yourselves.

adhkar.title: Aammar said: ‘Three characteristics, whoever combines them, has...
adhkar.arabicText: ثَلاَثٌ مَنْ جَمَعَهُنَّ فَقَدْ جَمَعَ الْإِيمَانَ: الْإِنْصَافُ مِنْ نَفْسِكَ، وَبَذْلُ السَّلاَمِ لِلْعَالَمِ، وَالْإِنْفَاقُ مِنَ الإِقْتَارِ.
adhkar.transliteration: thalathun man jama'ahunna faqad jama'a aliiymaana: aliinsaafu min nafsika, wabadhlu alssalami lil'aalami, waaliinfaaqu mina aliiqtaari
adhkar.translation: Aammar said: ‘Three characteristics, whoever combines them, has completed his faith: to be just, to spread greetings to all people and to spend (charitably. out of the little you have.

adhkar.title: Aabdullah Ibn Aamr reported that a man asked...
adhkar.arabicText: وَعَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُمَرَ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمَا: أنَّ رَجُلاً سَأَلَ النَّبِيَّ صلى الله عليه وسلم أيُّ الْإِسْلاَمِ خَيْرٌ قَالَ: (تُطْعِمُ الطَّعَامَ، وَتَقْرأُ السَّلاَمَ عَلَى مَنْ عَرَفْتَ وَمَنْ لَمْ تَعْرِفْ)).
adhkar.transliteration: Wa'an 'abdi alllahi bni 'umara radiya alllahu 'anhumaa: anna rajulaan saala alnnabiyya sla Allah 'lyh wslm ayyu aliislami khayrun qaala: ((tut'imu altta'aama, wataqrau alssalama 'alaa man 'arafta waman lam ta'rif
adhkar.translation: Aabdullah Ibn Aamr reported that a man asked the Prophet : ‘Which Islam is the best?’. He replied: Feed (the poor), and greet those whom you know as well as those whom you do not.

adhkar.title: When the people of the Book greet you...
adhkar.arabicText: إذَا سَلَّمَ عَلَيْكُمْ أَهْلُ الْكِتَابِ فَقُولُوا: وَعَلَيْكُمْ.
adhkar.transliteration: idhaa sallama 'alaykum ahlu alkitaabi faquwluwa: wa'alaykum
adhkar.translation: When the people of the Book greet you, reply by saying: And upon you.)

adhkar.title: If you hear the crow of a rooster...
adhkar.arabicText: إِذَا سَمِعْتُمْ صِيَاحَ الدِّيَكَةِ فَاسْأَلُوا اللَّهَ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ؛ فَإِنَّهَا رَأَتْ مَلَكاً وَإِذَا سَمِعْتُمْ نَهِيقَ الْحِمَارِ فَتَعَوَّذُوا بِاللَّهِ مِنَ الشَّيطَانِ؛ فَإِنَّهُ رَأَى شَيْطَاناً.
adhkar.transliteration: iidhaa sami'tum siyaaha alddiyakahi faasaluwa alllaha min fadlihi; faiinnahaa raat malakaan waiidhaa sami'tum nahiyqa alhimaari fata'awwadhuwa bialllahi mina alshhaytaani; faiinnahu raaa shaytaanaan
adhkar.translation: If you hear the crow of a rooster, ask Allah for his bounty for it has seen an angel and if you hear the braying of an ass, seek refuge in Allah for it has seen a devil.

adhkar.title: If you hear the barking of dogs or...
adhkar.arabicText: إِذَا سَمِعْتُمْ نُبَاحَ الْكِلاَبِ وَنَهِيقَ الْحَمِيرِ بِاللَّيْلِ فَتَعَوَّذُوا بِاللَّهِ مِنْهُنَّ؛ فَإِنَّهُنَّ يَرَيْنَ مَا لاَ تَرَوْنَ.
adhkar.transliteration: iidhaa sami'tum nubaaha alkilabi wanahiyqa alhamiyri bialllayli fata'awwadhuwa bialllahi minhunna; faiinnahunna yarayna maa la tarawna
adhkar.translation: If you hear the barking of dogs or the braying of asses at night, seek refuge in Allah for they see what you do not.

adhkar.title: O Allah, to any believer whom I have...
adhkar.arabicText: قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم: (اللَّهُمَّ فَأَيُّمَا مُؤْمِنٍ سَبَبْتُهُ فَاجْعَلْ ذَلِكَ لَهُ قُرْبَةً إِلَيْكَ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ)).
adhkar.transliteration: Allahumma fa-ayyuma mu/minin sababtuhu fajAAal thalika lahu qurbatan ilayka yawmal-qiyamah
adhkar.translation: O Allah, to any believer whom I have insulted, let that be cause to draw him near to You on the Day of Resurrection.

adhkar.title: He said: ‘If anyone of you is impelled...
adhkar.arabicText: قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم: (إِذَا كَانَ أَحَدُكُم مَادِحاً صَاحِبَهُ لاَ مَحَالَةَ فَلْيَقُلْ: أَحْسِبُ فُلاَناً وَاللَّهُ حَسِيبُهُ، وَلاَ أُزَكِّي عَلَى اللَّهِ أَحَداً، أَحْسِبُهُ – إِنْ كَانَ يَعْلَمُ ذَاكَ – كَذَا وَكَذَا)).
adhkar.transliteration: Qaala alnnabiyyu sla Allah 'lyh wslm: ((iidhaa kaana ahadukum maadihaan saahibahu la mahaalaha falyaqul: ahsibu fulanaan waalllahu hasiybuhu, wala auzakkiy 'alaa alllahi ahadaan, ahsibuhu – iin kaana ya'lamu dhaaka – kadhaa wakadhaa
adhkar.translation: He said: ‘If anyone of you is impelled to praise his brother, then he should say: ‘I deem so-and-so to be…and Allah is his reckoner…and I don’t praise anyone, putting it (i.e. my praising. forward, in front of Allah’s commendation, however I assume him so and so’…if he knows that of him.

adhkar.title: O Allah, do not call me to account...
adhkar.arabicText: اللَّهُمَّ لاَ تُؤَاخِذْنِي بِمَا يَقُولُونَ، وَاغْفِرْ لِي مَا لاَ يَعْلَمُونَ، [وَاجْعَلْنِي خَيْراً مِمَّا يَظُّنُّونَ].
adhkar.transliteration: Allaahumma laa tu'aakhithnee bimaa yaqooloona, waghfir lee maa laa ya'lamoona waj'alnee khayram-mimmaa yadhunnoon
adhkar.translation: O Allah, do not call me to account for what they say and forgive me for what they have no knowledge of [and make me better than they imagine].

adhkar.title: Here I am O Allah, (in response to...
adhkar.arabicText: لَبَّيْكَ اللَّهُمَّ لَبَّيْكَ، لَبَّيْكَ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَكَ لَبَّيْكَ، إِنَّ الْحَمْدَ، وَالنِّعْمَةَ، لَكَ وَالْمُلْكَ، لاَ شَرِيكَ لَكَ.
adhkar.transliteration: Labbaykal-lahumma labbayk, labbayka la shareeka laka labbayk, innal-hamda wanniAAmata laka walmulk, la shareeka lak
adhkar.translation: Here I am O Allah, (in response to Your call), here I am. Here I am, You have no partner, here I am. Verily all praise, grace and sovereignty belong to You. You have no partner.

adhkar.title: The Prophet circled the KaAAbah on a camel...
adhkar.arabicText: طَافَ النَّبيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم بِالْبَيْتِ عَلَى بَعِيرٍ كُلَّمَا أَتَى الرُّكْنَ أَشَارَ إِلَيْهِ بِشَيْءٍ عِنْدَهُ وَكَبَّرَ.
adhkar.transliteration: The Prophet circled the KaAAbah on a camel, every time he reached the black stone he would point to it with his staff and say: (Allahu akbar
adhkar.translation: The Prophet circled the KaAAbah on a camel, every time he reached the black stone he would point to it with his staff and say: Allah is the greatest)

adhkar.title: The Prophet PBUH used to say between the...
adhkar.arabicText: ﴿رَبَّنَا آتِنَا فِي الدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةً وَفِي الْآخِرَةِ حَسَنَةً وَقِنَا عَذَابَ النَّارِ﴾.
adhkar.transliteration: The Prophet PBUH used to say between the Yemeni corner and the black stone: (Rabbana atina fee alddunya hasanatan wafee al-akhirati hasanatan waqina AAathaba alnnar)Al-Baqarah: 201
adhkar.translation: The Prophet PBUH used to say between the Yemeni corner and the black stone: O our Lord, grant us the best in this life and the best in the next life, and protect us from the punishment of the Fire.)
adhkar.sourceReference: Abū Dāwud (#1892, 2/179), Aḥmad (3/411) and al-Baghawī in Sharḥ al-Sunnah (7/128). Al-Albānī declared it ḥasan in Ṣaḥīḥ Abī Dāwud (1/354). Sūrah al-Baqarah, 2:201

adhkar.title: Invocation to be recited while standing at Safa and Marwah
adhkar.arabicText: لَمَّا دَنَا النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم مِنَ الصَّفَا قَرَأَ: ﴿إِنَّ الصَّفَا وَالْمَرْوَةَ مِنْ شَعَآئِرِ اللَّهِ﴾ أَبْدَأُ بِمَا بَدَأَ اللَّهُ بِهِ). فَبَدَأَ بِالصَّفَا فَرَقِيَ عَلَيْهِ حَتَّى رَأَى الْبَيْتَ، فَاسْتَقْبَلَ الْقِبْلَةَ، فَوَحَّدَ اللَّهَ وَكبَّرَهُ وَقَالَ: (لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ، لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ، أَنْجَزَ وَعْدَهُ، وَنَصَرَ عَبْدَهُ، وَهَزَمَ الْأَحْزَابَ وَحْدَهُ، ثُمَّ دَعَا بَيْنَ ذلكَ. قَالَ مِثْلَ هَذَا ثَلاَثَ مَرَّاتٍ). الْحَدِيثُ. وَفِيهِ: (فَفَعَلَ عَلَى الْمَرْوَةِ كَمَا فَعَلَ عَلَى الصَّفَا)).
adhkar.transliteration: Lammaa danaa alnnabiyyu sla Allah 'lyh wslm mina alssafaa qaraa: iinna alssafaa waalmarwaha min sha'aaiiri alllahi abdaau bimaa badaa alllahu bihi)) fabadaa bialssafaa faraqiya 'alayhi hattaa raaa albayta, faastaqbala alqiblaha, fawahhada alllaha wakbbarahu waqaala: ((la iilaha iila alllahu wahdahu la shariyka lahu, lahu almulku walahu alhamdu wahuwa 'alaa kulli shay'in qadirun, la iilaha iila alllahu wahdahu, anjaza wa'dahu, wanasara 'abdahu, wahazama alahzaaba wahdahu, thumma da'aa bayna dhlka. qaala mithla hadhaa thalatha marraatin)) alhadiythu. wafiyhi: ((fafa'ala 'alaa almarwahi kamaa fa'ala 'alaa alssafaa
adhkar.translation: Jabir said when describing the Prophet's pilgrimage: '...and when he approached mount Safa he recited: Indeed Safa and Marwah are from the places of worship of Allah. I begin with what Allah began with. So he started with Safa and climbed it until he could see the KaAAbah, he then faced it and said: Allah is the greatest, Allah is the greatest, Allah is the greatest, and then he would say the following three times, making a supplication (one should make a personal supplication after each time): None has the right to be worshipped except Allah, alone, without partner. To Him belongs all sovereignty and praise and He is over all things omnipotent. None has the right to be worshipped except Allah alone. He fulfilled His promise, aided His Servant and single-handedly defeated the allies. (He would repeat this action at Marwah.)'

adhkar.title: The best of supplications is the supplication on...
adhkar.arabicText: قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم: (خَيْرُ الدُّعَاءِ دُعَاءُ يَوْمِ عَرَفَةَ، وَخَيْرُ مَا قُلْتُ أَنَا وَالنَّبيُّونَ مِنْ قَبْلِي: لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ)).
adhkar.transliteration: The best of supplications is the supplication on the day of AAarafah and the best which I and the Prophets before me have said (is): (La ilaha illal-lahu wahdahu la shareeka lah, lahul-mulku walahul-hamd, wahuwa AAala kulli shayin qadeer
adhkar.translation: The best of supplications is the supplication on the day of AAarafah and the best which I and the Prophets before me have said (is): None has the right to be worshipped except Allah, alone, without partner. To Him belongs all praise and sovereignty and He is over all things omnipotent.)

adhkar.title: The Prophet rode his camel, Al-Qaswa, until he...
adhkar.arabicText: رَكِبَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم الْقَصْوَاءَ حَتَّى أَتَى الْمَشْعَرَ الْحَرَامَ فَاسْتَقْبَلَ الْقِبْلَةَ (فَدَعَاهُ، وَكَبَّرَهُ، وَهَللَّهُ، وَوَحَّدَهُ. فَلَمْ يَزَلْ وَاقِفاً حَتَّى أَسْفَرَ جِدَّاً فَدَفَعَ قَبْلَ أَنْ تَطْلُعَ الشَّمسُ.
adhkar.transliteration: Rakiba alnnabiyyu sla Allah 'lyh wslm alqaswaa'a hattaa ataa almash'ara alharaama faastaqbala alqiblaha (fada'aahu, wakabbarahu, wahalllahu, wawahhadahu) falam yazal waaqifaan hattaa asfara jiddaaan fadafa'a qabla an tatlu'a alshhamsu
adhkar.translation: The Prophet rode his camel, Al-Qaswa, until he reached the sacred area (Al-Mash aril Haraam). Then he faced the Qiblah and invoked Allah, and repeatedly said the words: Allaahu Akbar (Allah is the Most Great), Allaahu Ahad (Allah is One), and Laa ilaaha illallaah (There is none worthy of worship but Allah). He remained stationary until the sky became yellow with the dawn, and then pressed on before sunrise.

adhkar.title: The Prophet PBUH said Allaahu 'Akbar (Allah is...
adhkar.arabicText: يُكَبِّرُ كُلَّمَا رَمَى بِحَصَاةٍ عِنْدَ الْجِمَارِ الثَّلاَثِ، ثُمَّ يَتَقَدَّمُ، ويَقِفُ يَدْعُو مُسْتَقْبِلَ الْقِبلَةِ، رَافِعاً يَدَيْهِ بَعْدَ الْجَمْرَةِ الْأُولَى وَالثَّانِيَةِ. أَمَّا جَمْرَةُ الْعَقَبَةِ فَيَرْمِيهَا وَيُكَبِّرُ عِنْدَ كُلِّ حَصَاةٍ وَيَنْصَرِفُ وَلاَ يَقِفُ عِنْدَهَا.
adhkar.transliteration: yukabbiru kullamaa ramaa bihasaahin 'inda aljimaari althhalathi, thumma yataqaddamu, wyaqifu yad'uw mustaqbila alqiblahi, raafi'aan yadayhi ba'da aljamrahi alauwlaa waalthhaaniyahi. ammaa jamrahu al'aqabahi fayarmiyhaa wayukabbiru 'inda kulli hasaahin wayansarifu wala yaqifu 'indahaa
adhkar.translation: The Prophet PBUH said Allaahu 'Akbar (Allah is the Most Great. with each pebble he threw at the three pillars. Then he went forward, stood facing the Qiblah and raised his hands and called upon Allah. That was after (stoning. the first and second pillar As for the third, he stoned it and called out Allahu Akbar with every pebble he threw but when he was finished he left without standing at it (for supplications).

adhkar.title: How perfect Allah is
adhkar.arabicText: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ!.
adhkar.transliteration: Subhanal-lah!
adhkar.translation: How perfect Allah is.

adhkar.title: Allahu akbar.
adhkar.arabicText: اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ!.
adhkar.transliteration: Allah akbaru
adhkar.translation: Allah is the greatest.

adhkar.title: The Prophet PBUH would prostrate in gratitude to...
adhkar.arabicText: كَانَ النَّبيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم إِذَا أَتَاهُ أَمْرٌ يَسُرُّهُ أَوْ يُسَرُّ بِهِ خَرَّ سَاجِداً شُكْراً لِلَّهِ تَبَارَكَ وَتَعَالَى.
adhkar.transliteration: kaana alnnabyyu sla Allah 'lyh wslm iidhaa ataahu amrun yasurruhu aw yusarru bihi kharra saajidaan shukraan lillahi tabaaraka wata'aalaa
adhkar.translation: The Prophet PBUH would prostrate in gratitude to Allah upon receiving news which pleased him or which caused pleasure.

adhkar.title: Place your hand at the site of the...
adhkar.arabicText: ضَعْ يَدَكَ عَلَى الَّذِي تَألَّمَ مِنْ جَسَدِكَ وَقُلْ: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ، ثَلاَثاً، وَقُلْ سَبْعَ مَرَّاتٍ: أَعُوذُ بِاللَّهِ وَقُدْرَتِهِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا أَجِدُ وَأُحَاذِرُ.
adhkar.transliteration: Place your hand at the site of the pain and say: (Bismil-lah) the supplicate
adhkar.translation: Place your hand at the site of the pain and say: In the name of Allah(three times). the supplicate seven times: I take refuge in Allah and within His omnipotence from the evil that I feel and am wary of.)

adhkar.title: If you see anything of your brother that...
adhkar.arabicText: إِذَا رَأَى أَحَدُكُم مِنْ أَخِيهِ، أَوْ مِنْ نَفْسِهِ، أَوْ مِنْ مَالِهِ مَا يُعْجِبُهُ [فَلْيَدْعُ لَهُ بِالْبَرَكَةِ] فَإِنَّ الْعَيْنَ حَقٌّ.
adhkar.transliteration: iidhaa raaa ahadukum min akhiyhi, aw min nafsihi, aw min maalihi maa yu'jibuhu faiinna al'ayna haqqun
adhkar.translation: If you see anything of your brother that pleases you, or of his person or of his property [then ask Allah to bless him in it] for the envious eye is real.

adhkar.title: What to say when you feel frightened
adhkar.arabicText: لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ!.
adhkar.transliteration: La ilaha illal-lah
adhkar.sourceReference: Abū Dāwud (#3116, 3/190). Also See Ṣaḥīḥ al-Jāmiʿ (#6479, 5/432)

adhkar.title: In the name of Allah, and Allah is...
adhkar.arabicText: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ وَاللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ [اللَّهُمَّ مِنْكَ وَلَكَ] اللَّهُمَّ تَقَبَّلْ مِنِّي.
adhkar.transliteration: Bismil-lah wallahu akbar, allahumma minka walak, allahumma taqabbal minnee
adhkar.translation: In the name of Allah, and Allah is the greatest. O Allah, (it is. from You and belongs to You, O Allah, accept this from me.
adhkar.sourceReference: Muslim (#1966, 3/1557) and al-Bayhaqī (9/287). What is between the brackets is from al-Bayhaqī and others

adhkar.title: I take refuge within Allah’s perfect words which...
adhkar.arabicText: أَعُوذُ بكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ التَّامَّاتِ الَّتِي لاَ يُجَاوِزُهُنَّ بَرٌّ وَلاَ فَاجِرٌ: مِنْ شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ، وَبَرَأَ وَذَرَأَ، وَمِنْ شَرِّ مَا يَنْزِلُ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ، وَمِنْ شَرِّ مَا يَعْرُجُ فيهَا، وَمِنْ شَرِّ مَا ذَرَأَ فِي الْأَرْضِ، وَمِنْ شَرِّ مَا يَخْرُجُ مِنْهَا، وَمِنْ شَرِّ فِتَنِ اللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهَارِ، وَمِنْ شَرِّ كُلِّ طَارِقٍ إِلاَّ طَارِقاً يَطْرُقُ بِخَيْرٍ يَا رَحْمَنُ.
adhkar.transliteration: aAAoothu bikalimatil-lahit-tammat, allatee la yujawizuhunna barrun wala fajir min sharri ma khalaq, wabaraa watharaa, wamin sharri ma yanzilu minas-sama/, wamin sharri ma yaAAruju feeha, wamin sharri ma tharaa fil-ard, wamin sharri ma yakhruju minha, wamin sharri fitnanil-layli wannahar, wamin sharri kulli tariqin illa tariqan yatruqu bikhayrin ya Rahman
adhkar.translation: I take refuge within Allah’s perfect words which no righteous or unrighteous person can transgress, from all the evil that He has created, made and originated. (I take refuge. from the evil that descends from the sky and the evil that rises up to it. (I take refuge. from the evil that is spread on Earth and the evil that springs from her, and I take refuge from the evil of the tribulations of night and day, and the evil of one who visits at night except the one who brings good, O Merciful One.

adhkar.title: The Messenger of Allah said: ‘By Allah, I...
adhkar.arabicText: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم: (وَاللَّهِ إِنِّي لأَسْتَغفِرُ اللَّهَ وَأَتُوبُ إِلَيْهِ فِي الْيَوْمِ أَكْثَرَ مِنْ سَبْعِينَ مَرَّةٍ)).
adhkar.transliteration: Qaala rasuwlu alllahi sla Allah 'lyh wslm: ((waalllahi iinniy lastaghfiru alllaha waatuwbu iilayhi fi alyawmi akthara min sab'iyna marrahin
adhkar.translation: The Messenger of Allah said: ‘By Allah, I seek forgiveness and repent to Allah, more than seventy times a day.

adhkar.title: He also said: ‘O People, Repent! Verily I...
adhkar.arabicText: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: (يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ تُوبُوا إِلَى اللَّهِ فَإِنِّي أَتُوبُ فِي الْيَوْمِ إِلَيْهِ مِائَةَ مَرَّةٍ)).
adhkar.transliteration: Waqaala sla Allah 'lyh wslm: ((yaa ayyuhaa alnnaasu tuwbuwa iilaa alllahi faiinniy atuwbu fi alyawmi iilayhi miaiaha marrahin
adhkar.translation: He also said: ‘O People, Repent! Verily I repent to Allah, a hundred times a day.

adhkar.title: He also said: ‘Whoever says:(I seek Allah’s forgiveness...
adhkar.arabicText: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: (مَنْ قَالَ أَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ الْعَظيمَ الَّذِي لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ هُوَ الْحَيُّ القَيّوُمُ وَأَتُوبُ إِلَيهِ، غَفَرَ اللَّهُ لَهُ وَإِنْ كَانَ فَرَّ مِنَ الزَّحْفِ)).
adhkar.transliteration: He also said:
adhkar.translation: He also said: ‘Whoever says: I seek Allah’s forgiveness, besides whom, none has the right to be worshipped except He, The Ever Living, The Self-Subsisting and Supporter of all, and I turn to Him in repentance. Allah would forgive him even if he was one who fled during the advance of an army.

adhkar.title: He said: ‘The nearest the Lord comes to...
adhkar.arabicText: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: (أَقْرَبُ مَا يَكُونُ الرَّبُّ مِنَ الْعَبْدِ فِي جَوْفِ اللَّيْلِ الآخِرِ فَإِنِ اسْتَطَعْتَ أَنْ تَكُونَ مِمَّنْ يَذْكُرُ اللَّهَ فِي تِلْكَ السَّاعَةِ فَكُنْ)).
adhkar.transliteration: Waqaala sla Allah 'lyh wslm: ((aqrabu maa yakuwnu alrrabbu mina al'abdi fi jawfi alllayli alakhiri faiini astata'ta an takuwna mimman yadhkuru alllaha fi tilka alssaa'ahi fakun
adhkar.translation: He said: ‘The nearest the Lord comes to His servant is in the middle of the night, so if you are able to be of those who remember Allah at that time, then be so.

adhkar.title: He also said: ‘The nearest a servant is...
adhkar.arabicText: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: (أَقْرَبُ مَا يَكُونُ الْعَبْدُ مِنْ رَبِّهِ وَهُوَ سَاجِدٌ فَأَكثِرُوا الدُّعَاءَ)).
adhkar.transliteration: Waqaala sla Allah 'lyh wslm: ((aqrabu maa yakuwnu al'abdu min rabbihi wahuwa saajidun faakthiruwa alddu'aa'a
adhkar.translation: He also said: ‘The nearest a servant is to his Lord is when he is prostrating, so supplicate much therein.

adhkar.title: He also said: ‘verily my heart becomes preoccupied...
adhkar.arabicText: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: (إِنَّهُ لَيُغَانُ عَلَى قَلْبِي وَإِنِّي لأَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ فِي الْيَوْمِ مِائَةَ مَرَّةٍ)).
adhkar.transliteration: Waqaala sla Allah 'lyh wslm: ((iinnahu layughaanu 'alaa qalbiy waiinniy lastaghfiru alllaha fi alyawmi miaiaha marrahin
adhkar.translation: He also said: ‘verily my heart becomes preoccupied, and verily I seek Allah’s forgiveness a hundred times a day. (preoccupied: i.e. in a state of ‘forgetfulness’. The Prophet always used to increase in his remembrance of his Lord, in attaining a nearness to Allah and having consciousness of Allah to the extent that if this intensity lessened in anyway, he would regard it as a sin and would then race to seek forgiveness from Allah.)

adhkar.title: Whoever says:(How perfect Allah is and I praise...
adhkar.arabicText: قَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم مَنْ قَالَ: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ فِي يَوْمٍ مِائَةَ مَرَّةٍ حُطَّتْ خَطَايَاهُ وَلَوْ كَانَتْ مِثْلَ زَبَدِ الْبَحْر)).
adhkar.transliteration: Whoever says:(Subhanal-lahi wabihamdih.) a hundred times during the day, his sins are wiped away, even if they are like the foam of the sea
adhkar.translation: Whoever says: How perfect Allah is and I praise Him. a hundred times during the day, his sins are wiped away, even if they are like the foam of the sea.

adhkar.title: Abu Hurayrah reported that the Messenger of Allah...
adhkar.arabicText: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: (مَنْ قَالَ لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ، وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ، وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ عَشْرَ مِرَارٍ، كَانَ كَمَنْ أَعْتَقَ أَرْبَعَةَ أَنْفُسٍ مِنْ وَلَدِ إِسْمَاعِيلَ)).
adhkar.transliteration: Abu Hurayrah reported that the Messenger of Allah said:
adhkar.translation: Abu Hurayrah reported that the Messenger of Allah said: ‘Whoever says: None has the right to be worshipped except Allah, alone, without partner. To Him belongs all sovereignty and praise and He is over all things omnipotent. a hundred times during the day, has the reward of freeing ten slaves, a hundred good deeds are recorded for him and a hundred bad deeds are wiped away and he has gained refuge from the devil that day until evening and none shall come with anything better except someone who has done more.

adhkar.title: Aboo Hurayrah reported that the Messenger of Allah...
adhkar.arabicText: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: (كَلِمَتَانِ خَفِيفَتَانِ عَلَى اللِّسَانِ، ثَقِيلَتَانِ فِي الْمِيزَانِ، حَبِيبَتَانِ إِلَى الرَّحْمَنِ: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ، سُبْحانَ اللَّهِ الْعَظِيمِ)).
adhkar.transliteration: Aboo Hurayrah reported that the Messenger of Allah said:’(There are) Two words, (which are) light on the tongue, heavy on the Scale and beloved to The Most Gracious: (Subhanal-lahi wabihamdih, wasubhanal-lahil-AAatheem
adhkar.translation: Aboo Hurayrah reported that the Messenger of Allah said:’(There are. Two words, (which are. light on the tongue, heavy on the Scale and beloved to The Most Gracious: How perfect Allah is and I praise Him. How perfect Allah is, The Supreme.)

adhkar.title: Aboo Hurayrah reported that the Messenger of Allah...
adhkar.arabicText: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: (لَأَنْ أَقُولَ سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ، وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ، وَلاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ، وَاللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، أَحَبُّ إِلَيَّ مِمَّا طَلَعَتْ عَلَيْهِ الشَّمسُ)).
adhkar.transliteration: Aboo Hurayrah reported that the Messenger of Allah said:
adhkar.translation: Aboo Hurayrah reported that the Messenger of Allah said: ‘Saying: How perfect Allah is, and all praise is for Allah. None has the right to be worshipped except Allah, and Allah is the greatest. is more beloved to me than everything the sun has risen over.

adhkar.title: Sa’d said: ‘We were sitting with the Messenger...
adhkar.arabicText: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: (أَيَعْجِزُ أَحَدُكُم أَنْ يَكْسِبَ كُلَّ يَوْمٍ أَلْفَ حَسَنَةٍ). فَسَأَلَهُ سَائِلٌ مِنْ جُلَسَائِهِ كَيْفَ يَكْسِبُ أَحَدُنَا أَلْفَ حَسَنَةٍ؟ قَالَ: (يُسَبِّحُ مِائَةَ تَسْبِيحَةٍ، فَيُكتَبُ لَهُ أَلْفُ حَسَنَةٍ أَوْ يُحَطُّ عَنْهُ أَلْفُ خَطِيئَةٍ)).
adhkar.transliteration: Sa’d said:
adhkar.translation: Sa’d said: ‘We were sitting with the Messenger of Allah , and he said: ‘Are any of you unable to gain a thousand good deeds each day?’ Somebody then asked him : How does one achieve a thousand good deeds? He replied: ‘He should say: How perfect Allah is. one hundred times, for a thousand good deeds are recorded for him or a thousand bad deeds are wiped away.

adhkar.title: Jabbir related that the Prophet said: ‘Whoever says...
adhkar.arabicText: مَنْ قَالَ: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ الْعَظِيمِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ غُرِسَتْ لَهُ نَخْلَةٌ فِي الْجَنَّةِ.
adhkar.transliteration: Jabbir related that the Prophet said:
adhkar.translation: Jabbir related that the Prophet said: ‘Whoever says: How perfect Allah is. The Supreme, and I praise Him. a palm tree is planted for him in paradise.

adhkar.title: AAabdullah Ibn Qays related that the Prophet said...
adhkar.arabicText: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: (يَا عَبْدَ اللَّهِ بْنَ قَيْسٍ أَلاَ أَدُلُّكَ عَلَى كَنْزٍ مِنْ كُنُوزِ الْجَنَّةِ). ؟ فَقُلْتُ: بَلَى يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، قَالَ: (قُلْ لاَ حَوْلَ وَلاَ قُوَّةَ إِلاَّ بِاللَّهِ)).
adhkar.transliteration: AAabdullah Ibn Qays related that the Prophet said to him:
adhkar.translation: AAabdullah Ibn Qays related that the Prophet said to him: ‘ O ‘AAabdullah Ibn Qays, shall I not inform you of a treasure from the treasures of paradise?’ He then said: ‘Say: There is no might nor power except with Allah.)

adhkar.title: the most beloved words to Allah are four...
adhkar.arabicText: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: (أَحَبُّ الْكَلاَمِ إِلَى اللَّهِ أَرْبَعٌ: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ، وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ، وَلاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ، وَاللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، لاَ يَضُرُّكَ بِأَيِّهِنَّ بَدَأتَ)).
adhkar.transliteration: the most beloved words to Allah are four: (Subhanal-lah, walhamdu lillah, wala ilaha illal-lah, wallahu akbar.)(it does not matter which of them you start with
adhkar.translation: the most beloved words to Allah are four: How perfect Allah is, all praise is for Allah. None has the right to be worshipped except Allah and Allah is the greatest.) (it does not matter which of them you start with.)

adhkar.title: SaAAd Ibn Abee Waqqas narrated that a man...
adhkar.arabicText: جَاءَ أَعْرَابِيٌّ إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم فَقَالَ: عَلِّمْنِي كَلاماً أَقُولُهُ: قَالَ: (قُلْ: لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَهُ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ كَبِيراً، وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ كَثِيراً، سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ رَبِّ العَالَمِينَ، لاَ حَوْلَ وَلاَ قُوَّةَ إِلاَّ بِاللَّهِ الْعَزِيزِ الْحَكِيمِ). قَالَ: فَهَؤُلاَءِ لِرَبِّي، فَمَا لِي؟ قَالَ: (قُلْ: اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لِي، وَارْحَمْنِي، وَاهْدِنِي، وَارْزُقْنِي)).
adhkar.transliteration: SaAAd Ibn Abee Waqqas narrated that a man came to the Messenger of Allah and said to him:
adhkar.translation: SaAAd Ibn Abee Waqqas narrated that a man came to the Messenger of Allah and said to him: ‘Teach me something which I should say?’ He said: ‘Say: None has the right to be worshipped except Allah, alone without partener. Allah is most great and much praise is for Allah. How perfect Allah is, Lord of the worlds. There is no might nor power except with Allah, The Exalted in might, The Wise. the man then said: ‘These are for my Lord, and what is for me?’ He replied: ‘Say: O Allah, forgive me, have mercy upon me, guide me and grant me sustenance.)

adhkar.title: Tariq Al-AshjaAAee said: ‘When someone would embrace Islam...
adhkar.arabicText: كَانَ الرَّجُلُ إِذَا أَسْلَمَ عَلَّمَهُ النَّبيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم الصَّلاَةَ ثُمَّ أَمَرَهُ أَنْ يَدْعُوَ بِهَؤُلاَءِ الْكَلِمَاتِ: (اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرِ لِي، وَارْحَمْنِي، وَاهْدِنِي، وَعَافِنِي وَارْزُقْنِي)).
adhkar.transliteration: Tariq Al-AshjaAAee said:
adhkar.translation: Tariq Al-AshjaAAee said: ‘When someone would embrace Islam, the Prophet would teach him how to perform prayer and then order him to supplicate with the following words: O Allah, forgive me, have mercy upon me, guide me, give me health and grant me sustenance.)

adhkar.title: Jabir Ibn ‘AAabdullah related that the Messenger of...
adhkar.arabicText: إِنَّ أَفْضَلَ الدُّعَاءِ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ، وَأَفْضَلَ الذِّكْرِ لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ.
adhkar.transliteration: Jabir Ibn
adhkar.translation: Jabir Ibn 'AAabdullah related that the Messenger of Allah said: 'Verily, the best supplication is: All praise is for Allah, and indeed, the best form of remembrance is: None has the right to be worshipped except Allah.'
adhkar.sourceReference: Al-Tirmidhī (#3383, 5/462), Ibn Mājah (#3800, 2/1249), and al-Hākim (1/503) and he authenticated it and al-Dhahabī agreed with him. Also see Ṣaḥīḥ al-Jāmiʿ (#1104, 1/362)

adhkar.title: The good deeds which endure are 'Glorified is...
adhkar.arabicText: الباقيات الصالحات : سبحان الله والحمد لله ، ولا إله إلا الله ،والله أكبر ،و لا حول ولا قوة إلا بالله
adhkar.transliteration: The good deeds which endure are 'Subhaanallaah.', and 'Walhamdulillaah.', and 'Wa laa ilaaha illallaah'., and 'Wallaahu Akbar',and 'Wa laa hawla wa laa guwawala illaa billaah'
adhkar.translation: The good deeds which endure are 'Glorified is Allah', and 'The praise is for Allah', and 'but There is none worthy of worship Allah', and 'Allah is the Most Great', and 'There is no power and no might except by Allah'

adhkar.title: Tasbeeh, it means here, to say: (Subhanal-lah, alhamdu...
adhkar.arabicText: عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو رضي الله عنه قَالَ: (رَأَيْتُ النَّبيَّ صلى الله عليه وسلم يَعْقِدُ التَّسْبِيحَ). وفي زيادةٍ: (بِيَمِينِهِ)).
adhkar.transliteration: 'an 'abdi alllahi bni 'amrinw rdy Allah 'nh qaala: ((raaytu alnnabyya sla Allah 'lyh wslm ya'qidu alttasbiyha)) wfy zyadhin: ((biyamiynihi
adhkar.translation: Tasbeeh, it means here, to say: Subhanal-lah, alhamdu lillah, Allahu akbar.) (AAabdullah Ibn AAamr said: ’I saw the prophet make tasbeeh with his right hand.)

adhkar.title: When evening descends, bring your children indoors for...
adhkar.arabicText: قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم ((إِذَا كَانَ جُنْحُ اللَّيْلِ – أَوْ أَمْسَيْتُم – فَـكُفُّوا صِبْيانَـكُم، فَإِنَّ الشَّيَاطِينَ تَنْتَشِرُ حِينَئِذٍ، فَإِذَا ذَهَبَ سَاعَةٌ مِنَ اللَّيلِ فَخَلُّوهُمْ، وَأَغْلِقُوا الأَبْوَابَ وَاذْكُرُوا اسْمَ اللَّهِ؛ فَإِنَّ الشَّيطَانَ لاَ يَفْتَحُ بَاباً مُغلَقاً، وَأَوْكُوا قِرَبَكُمْ، وَاذْكُرُوا اسْمَ اللَّهِ، وَخَمِّرُوا آنِيَتَكُم، وَاذْكُرُوا اسْمَ اللَّهِ، وَلَوْ أَنْ تَعْرُضُوا عَلَيْهَا شَيْئاً، وَأَطْفِئُوا مَصَابِيحَكُمْ)).
adhkar.transliteration: Qaala alnnabiyyu sla Allah 'lyh wslm ((iidhaa kaana junhu alllayli – aw amsaytum – faـkuffuwa sibyanaـkum, faiinna alshhayaatiyna tantashiru hiynaiidhin, faiidhaa dhahaba saa'ahun mina alllayli fakhalluwhum, waaghliquwa alabwaaba waadhkuruwa asma alllahi; faiinna alshhaytaana la yaftahu baabaan mughlaqaan, waawkuwa qirabakum, waadhkuruwa asma alllahi, wakhammiruwa aniyatakum, waadhkuruwa asma alllahi, walaw an ta'ruduwa 'alayhaa shayiaan, waatfiiuwa masaabiyhakum
adhkar.translation: When evening descends, bring your children indoors for the devils scatter out at this hour. Then after the passing of an hour (i.e. the first hour. of the night, (you may. let them (the children. go. Close your doors while mentioning the Name of Allah, for the devil may not open a closed door. Fasten your waterskins mentioning the Name of Allah. Cover your eating vessels mentioning the Name of Allah even if you just put something over it, and extinguish your lamps. Peace and blessing be upon our Prophet, Muhammad, and upon his family and his Companions, all of them.
